package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for range_over_string_by_index: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ { c := s[i] }` on strings that should iterate runes
type CaseRangeOverStringByIndexDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseRangeOverStringByIndexModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseRangeOverStringByIndexRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseRangeOverStringByIndexModel
}

type CaseRangeOverStringByIndexService struct {
    repo *CaseRangeOverStringByIndexRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseRangeOverStringByIndexAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeRangeOverStringByIndex(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseRangeOverStringByIndexDTO) error {
    focus := "range_over_string_by_index"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    range_over_string_index_negative := map[string]int{"range": 1, "over": 2, "string": 3, "index": 4}
    range_over_string_index_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    range_negative_0 := range_over_string_index_negative["range"] + len(input)
    _ = range_negative_0
    over_negative_1 := range_over_string_index_negative["over"] + len(input)
    _ = over_negative_1
    string_negative_2 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = string_negative_2
    index_negative_3 := range_over_string_index_negative["index"] + len(input)
    _ = index_negative_3
    return nil
}
